scope.family: Goal 16 graph-CZ encoded bridge dynamics plus a built-in coherent north/south screen router in one declared finite channel family
scope.screen_system: one logical payload qubit routed to complementary qutrit erasure screens
scope.atlas_bound: all simple graphs, all mouth permutations, and screen probabilities {0,0.5,1} for m<=3

core_claim: A single declared finite dynamics record derives the mouth map, right-mouth graph-CZ interaction, dressed observer algebra, exact bridge transfer, north/south screen channels, and the equal-bare-area recovery/area-analogue transition.

exact_theorem_style_claims.claim: The encoded bridge preparation, graph-CZ interaction, inverse bridge decoder, and north/south screen router are packaged as one finite dynamics record.
Full-block MI recovers pi, Pauli-correlation tomography recovers G, and CZ_G conjugation reconstructs the dressed observer algebra.
Inverse CZ_G plus recovered pi restores exact bridge transfer; wrong-mouth and mouth-blind controls fail.
North/south screen keep probabilities are read from the same dynamics' Kraus operators and determine both recovery ordering and the finite quantum-area analogue.
Dropping the built-in screen channels back to static bridge-state data restores the no-go: opposite screen-channel completions share the same static signature.
An external bare-area bias not produced by the unified channel breaks the recovery/area transition match.

bounded_exhaustive_evidence.scope: m=3 path graph, twisted pairing, north probability 0.75
all simple graphs, all mouth permutations, and screen probabilities 0,0.5,1 for m<=3

controls.claim: Identity activation on the twisted representative has capacity 1<3 after interaction removal.
The mouth-blind scrambling control has zero structured named-mouth capacity.
Static bridge-state diagnostics alone remain compatible with opposite north/south recovery winners.
A bare area bias shifts the area crossing to 0.375 while recovery remains at 0.5.

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the Goal 17 inseparable bridge-screen dynamics certificate.
Regression check for the Goal 17 certificate.
Validate the machine-readable Goal 17 certificate index.

known_vs_new.known_derived: Stabilizer graph-CZ bridge codes, Pauli-correlation stabilizer recovery, Bell transfer checks, coherent erasure-channel isometries, and Choi/Kraus diagnostics.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: Packages bridge reconstruction and screen recovery into one finite dynamics benchmark, with controls showing exactly what becomes an overclaim if screen dynamics or area terms are appended externally.

limitations: Finite stabilizer/QEC and dense-channel benchmark only. The screen is an explicit qutrit erasure isometry, not continuum geometry, de Sitter, dS/CFT, or a chaotic many-body wormhole.
